kept.text:
Just crossed $10k earned on Contra😍✨️

Not from one big project.
Not overnight.

but one thing that made the biggest difference?
🔻 Referrals and repeat clients.

A good number of these projects didn’t come from applying to jobs.

They came from past clients coming back or sending someone my way.

That only happens when: 

✅️ You deliver what you promised
✅️ You communicate well
✅️ You make the process easy for the client

Early on, it’s easy to focus only on getting new clients.

But keeping clients and doing work they’re happy to talk about matters just as much.

That’s what compounds.

If you’re freelancing, focus on doing work people remember🤝

Still early. More to build. Going for more✔️

kept.text:
$18k in sales first month, store built in one weekend

someone used claude to create the entire shopify catalog: titles, descriptions, pricing, tags, collections, navigation

50 products structured and uploaded without touching a spreadsheet

defined the logic once, claude executed across everything

traditional way to build a 50-product shopify store:

> hire a VA or content manager
> write 50 product descriptions manually
> research keywords for each listing
> organize collections and tags
> set up navigation and categories
> upload everything one by one
> 2-3 weeks minimum, $500-2000 in labor

his way:

> connected claude to shopify documentation and APIs
> wrote one PRD (product requirement document) defining brand voice, pricing logic, tag structure
> fed raw product data and images
> claude generated everything: titles, descriptions, tags, product types
> auto-assigned products to collections
> organized store structure and navigation

time spent: one weekend

the store launched monday morning

by friday: first $4k in orders

by end of month: $18k total sales

his secret wasn’t the products - it was the system

most people treat claude like a chatbot, asking one question at a time

he treated it like an employee with a playbook

the PRD told claude exactly how to name products, what tone to use, which price tiers to follow, how to structure tags for SEO

claude didn’t guess - it followed the system

when he wanted to add 20 more products next month, same process
feed data, claude executes, store updates

no retraining, no explaining from scratch
the playbook already exists

he’s now testing the same system on amazon merch and etsy digital

same product ideas, adapted for each platform

claude rewrites listings for how buyers search on each marketplace

one idea becomes three revenue streams

the store still runs, he still checks quality and approves listings

but the 80% of repetitive work - writing, organizing, structuring - is gone

kept.text:
do you understand what just happened to the e-commerce soul..

They replaced an entire 5-person team: content manager, designer, assistant, analyst, and dev, with a single AI agent on the morning of a $127,000 budget review.

The Claude Code shift was nuclear: 
→ research, SEO, and copywriting are no longer human roles 
→ one prompt executes a workflow across 5 different platforms (Amazon, Etsy, Shopify, etc.) 
→ the "operational side" that kills most startups has been automated out of existence

Gone.. Today.. While agencies were still arguing about "AI quality."

The $127K/year payroll didn't need to be optimized.
Claude Code proved it was obsolete.

For those new here, I’m Abiodun😊

I design and redesign Shopify websites for eCommerce brands that want more than just a “nice-looking” store.

The focus is simple:

✅️ Clear structure, better user experience, and product pages that actually convert visitors into buyers.

I also share what I’ve learned from freelancing. 

✔️ How I get clients, structure projects, and grow consistently without relying on luck.

Most of my posts are based on real work, real results, and lessons from actual client projects.

If you run an eCommerce brand or you’re building a freelance career, you’ll find something useful here.

Follow along🤝

kept.text:
Most e-commerce projects don't fail because of a bad idea

They fail because one person can't handle research, listings, SEO images, and pricing all at once

Claude Code doesn't replace your judgment
It removes everything that was eating your time

What actually works: Claude Code

Not (ask AI to write a description)

Build repeatable workflows once, then run them everywhere

>> Amazon Merch: upload design>> full listing keywords
>>Etsy Digital: drop a bestseller link>> get gap analysis + improved 
>> Shopify: raw product data >> full catalog, copy

One good listing prompt runs for months
You refine it reuse it, scale it

With Claude everything gets faster

kept.text:
I love Tobi Lütke but lets be honest, we have all heard him on several podcasts.

This episode is @tobi like you have never seen before. Spicy, fun and leaning into controversy.

I mean wow, he did not shy away from hard topics:

- AI as a scapegoat for layoffs
- The dangers of irresponsible charitable giving
- Carney’s stance against a Trump administration being wrong
- Why Elon Musk deserves more credit

I condensed my notes from the discussion below for you:

1. The $160 Billion CEO Who Did Not Want to Be CEO
To run a truly product-driven company, you must be in control of the company itself because the needs of a product and a business often diverge. This requires passing the "marshmallow test" by prioritizing a three-year perspective over immediate gratification. Founders must be willing to endure "bad numbers" in the short term if they believe it is the only way to reach the right long-term destination.

2. The Best Engineers in Shopify Are Not Writing Code Anymore and the AI Does It for Them
AI now generates over 50% of the code at Shopify, and that number is steadily increasing. Many of the company’s best engineers have not written a single line of manual code since December 2023, as the release of advanced models like Opus changed the fundamental nature of the job. Engineering has shifted from manual execution to "steering" AI agents through high-level context engineering.

3. Why AI Is Being Used as a Scapegoat for Mass Layoffs
The current wave of industry layoffs is largely a result of "over-hiring" during the COVID era rather than displacement by AI. AI has become the "perfect scapegoat" for slow companies because it cannot fight back against the narrative that it is destroying jobs. In reality, AI is an incredible tool that improves productivity across every department when implemented correctly.

4. Why We Are About to Enter a Golden Age of Entrepreneurship
Entrepreneurship is the most "AI-safe" and "AI-benefiting" career path available today. AI acts as a "handy sidekick" or co-founder, allowing individuals to focus on their core ambitions while the technology handles various execution tasks. The barriers to entry are dissolving; you no longer need a family background in business to understand that business formation is something real humans can do.

5. Why We Need to Place More Not Less Scrutiny on Charitable Giving
Giving money is only virtuous if it yields results; prioritize what works over what sounds good. "Charity dollars" lack the "self-healing fitness function" provided by for-profit markets. Be skeptical of organizations that choose to opt out of market intelligence.

6. Why Will Governments Regulating the Technology We and Our Children Use Push Us Into the Hands of the Chinese?
Age limits won't stop kids from using AI; they will just drive them toward uncurated "donut" Chinese models. This risks exposing youth to a collectivist monoculture that censors historical truth. Such regulation trades individualistic enlightenment values for state control.

(links below)

kept.text:
Just connected claude to Shopify and was playing around with it.

Some very interesting insights.

1) Gurgaon is our most loyal customer base.

2) Mumbai does the highest volume (no surprise there).

quoted_tweet.text:
I have personally seen 6 brands restart growth using the "hudson method"

we are talking 5m a year brands to 100m a year brands.

the steps:

1- seed hundreds of creators on tik tok
small, new accounts, no one famous

2- pay them per video PLUS commission
BUT heavily incentives them to post LOTS of videos

bonuses for posting 100+ videos a month

3- take all the creative, load it into every ad channel
you just solved creative bottlenecks and unlocked infinite ad angles

4- scale ad spend on EVERYTHING, while still paying the creators commission on the ad sales

--
it isnt about tik tok shop sales.
it isnt about gmv max.

its all of it.
you get free cpms on tik tok.
you get unlimited content.
and it jump starts the whole business

more ads, more angles, more channels.

this is named after the legendary founder of comfrt, hudson. all hail the ugc king.

profile_bio.description: Got banned from Facebook Ads in 2019. Learned email to survive. $50M+. Now our clients' emails convert higher than 90% of their industry, according to Klaviyo.

kept.text:
A founder came to me with 15K subscribers and a channel that was basically dy*ing. We revived the channel on Video 6. 

Janson Smith had been posting on YouTube for 7 years. His topics were all over the place.

• Taxes
• Amazon
• Dropshipping
• Shopify
• Accounting
• TikTok Shop

Getting 800-1K views per video. We narrowed his entire content strategy to one topic.

First thing we fixed wasn't just the content… 

His videos had an audio-visual sync glitch that made them physically unwatchable. 

So first, I had him buy specific gear to fix it.

Then we got ruthless on strategy… 

He wanted to do TikTok Shop, Amazon, and Shopify. 

Me: “You can only pick one.” 

Him: “Alright, let's do TikTok Shop.” 

Me: “Let’s go even narrower.”

We landed on AI + TikTok Shop specifically, nothing else.

Video 6 hit 20K views. After that: 21K, 10K, 43K, and his latest video is pulling thousands within hours of posting. 

And now he’s approaching 20K subs in 3 months across 14 videos total.

His feedback to me last week: "Keep doing what you're doing, this is booking calls."

kept.text: Tobi is spiritually American

profile_bio.description: Economic Blitzkrieg. Never said it was a good bet, just a complex bet. Decoupling ™️ Long/Short Tech Investor. Will say retarded shit often. 🫡

quoted_tweet.text:
I love Tobi Lütke but lets be honest, we have all heard him on several podcasts.

This episode is @tobi like you have never seen before. Spicy, fun and leaning into controversy.

I mean wow, he did not shy away from hard topics:

- AI as a scapegoat for layoffs
- The dangers of irresponsible charitable giving
- Carney’s stance against a Trump administration being wrong
- Why Elon Musk deserves more credit

I condensed my notes from the discussion below for you:

1. The $160 Billion CEO Who Did Not Want to Be CEO
To run a truly product-driven company, you must be in control of the company itself because the needs of a product and a business often diverge. This requires passing the "marshmallow test" by prioritizing a three-year perspective over immediate gratification. Founders must be willing to endure "bad numbers" in the short term if they believe it is the only way to reach the right long-term destination.

2. The Best Engineers in Shopify Are Not Writing Code Anymore and the AI Does It for Them
AI now generates over 50% of the code at Shopify, and that number is steadily increasing. Many of the company’s best engineers have not written a single line of manual code since December 2023, as the release of advanced models like Opus changed the fundamental nature of the job. Engineering has shifted from manual execution to "steering" AI agents through high-level context engineering.

3. Why AI Is Being Used as a Scapegoat for Mass Layoffs
The current wave of industry layoffs is largely a result of "over-hiring" during the COVID era rather than displacement by AI. AI has become the "perfect scapegoat" for slow companies because it cannot fight back against the narrative that it is destroying jobs. In reality, AI is an incredible tool that improves productivity across every department when implemented correctly.

4. Why We Are About to Enter a Golden Age of Entrepreneurship
Entrepreneurship is the most "AI-safe" and "AI-benefiting" career path available today. AI acts as a "handy sidekick" or co-founder, allowing individuals to focus on their core ambitions while the technology handles various execution tasks. The barriers to entry are dissolving; you no longer need a family background in business to understand that business formation is something real humans can do.

5. Why We Need to Place More Not Less Scrutiny on Charitable Giving
Giving money is only virtuous if it yields results; prioritize what works over what sounds good. "Charity dollars" lack the "self-healing fitness function" provided by for-profit markets. Be skeptical of organizations that choose to opt out of market intelligence.

6. Why Will Governments Regulating the Technology We and Our Children Use Push Us Into the Hands of the Chinese?
Age limits won't stop kids from using AI; they will just drive them toward uncurated "donut" Chinese models. This risks exposing youth to a collectivist monoculture that censors historical truth. Such regulation trades individualistic enlightenment values for state control.

(links below)
